title: Stop Prop Drilling Your React Modals (Use react-call)
summary: react-call is a lightweight utility that enables calling React components as asynchronous functions, eliminating prop drilling and state management boilerplate. It uses Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow architectures to handle layout isolation and loading states in a linear pattern.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers react-call, a lightweight utility that lets React components be called as asynchronous functions, removing the prop drilling and state management boilerplate that modals typically require. It explains the library's architecture, including Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow, which handle layout isolation and loading states in a linear pattern. The takeaway: dialog-style components can be invoked imperatively rather than wired through intermediate component props and context.
